mechanism_narrative: A sustained upward trend in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signals expanding household income and consumption demand, which drives corporate revenue growth and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC falls below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive changes in PAYEMS are not followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 6.0%, the labor market enters a tight regime where wage pressures build and consumer spending strengthens, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: In months where UNRATE is below 6.0%, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of triggered instances, or the mean return is not significantly greater than zero (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is held below 0.25% and consumer prices are accelerating, deeply negative real interest rates force capital out of nominal fixed income and into equities, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL shows no significant coefficient (p > 0.05) in predicting 20-day returns, or the strategy yields negative mean returns during active periods.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days specifically during late-cycle expansions when unemployment is already low, because supply gains translate into margin expansion rather than merely filling idle capacity.
failure_condition: When UNRATE is below 6.0%, positive month-over-month changes in INDPRO are followed by negative 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in retail sales captures real-time consumer spending momentum; positive surprises in this high-frequency demand proxy lead to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days as earnings expectations reprice.
failure_condition: RSAFS monthly increases are followed by negative S&P 500 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances over a rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation captured by CPIAUCSL signals future monetary tightening and erodes real corporate earnings, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive month-over-month CPIAUCSL changes are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of observations, or Rank IC exceeds -0.03 over a 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in housing starts and retail sales reflects coordinated strength in interest-rate-sensitive and household consumption sectors, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint month-over-month increases in HOUST and RSAFS are followed by negative 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of co-occurring instances.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth above the economy's long-run potential signals overheating and input cost pressures that compress corporate margins, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When INDPRO exceeds 105, positive month-over-month changes are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of triggered months.
